notes: Boutique's core purpose: persist Codable & Sendable value types via Store<Item>.

evidence.note: public typealias StorableItem = Codable & Sendable — all stored items are Codable
Store<Item> persists Codable items to a memory cache and a storage engine
func insert(_ item: Item) async throws — persists a Codable item
Package.swift declares .iOS(.v17) platform floor

Surface digest resolves this decl present on linux (no os() conditions anywhere in the public surface), but the module unconditionally imports SwiftUI, Security, and Observation; Package.swift and README declare only iOS 17+/macOS 14+. Linux buildability unverified — honest answer is unknown.

public typealias StorableItem = Codable & Sendable — all stored items are Codable No explicit macCatalyst floor in Package.swift (manifest lists only iOS/macOS); symbol anchors are unconditional and the digest resolves them present on macCatalyst.

func insert(_ item: Item) async throws — persists a Codable item

notes: Disk persistence is delivered through the StorageEngine protocol from the Bodega dependency (pinned 2.1.3); Boutique's own surface drives it unconditionally.

evidence.note: init(storage: StorageEngine, cacheIdentifier:) — persists items to a memory cache and a storage engine (disk persistence via Bodega StorageEngine, e.g. SQLiteStorageEngine)
doc: 'Removes all items from the store's memory cache and storage engine'
init(storage: StorageEngine) — Identifiable convenience, same dual-layer disk persistence
README: 'dual-layered memory + disk caching architecture ... full offline storage'

notes: Caveat: the in-memory layer is always paired with a StorageEngine; the only purely transient store in the public surface (previewStore) is DEBUG-only. Confidence lowered accordingly — Boutique is not a standalone in-memory database.

evidence.note: var items: [Item] — items are held in and read synchronously from the Store's in-memory cache layer (dual-layer memory + disk architecture)
doc: 'persisting items to a memory cache' — memory layer is intrinsic to every Store
DEBUG-only transient Store that only holds items in memory (SwiftUI Previews); no files written to disk

notes: UserDefaults-backed @StoredValue (plus Keychain-backed @SecurelyStoredValue, recorded separately under storage.keychain).

evidence.note: @StoredValue property wrapper persists a single Item in UserDefaults
init(wrappedValue:key:storage: UserDefaults = .standard) — key-value storage keyed by String
func set(_ item: Item) — writes the value for the wrapper's key
func reset() — resets the stored value to its default
Package.swift declares .iOS(.v17) platform floor

notes: Implementation imports the Security framework (Apple platforms only in practice); linux left unknown per rules — no os() guard evidence of absence in the surface.

evidence.note: @SecurelyStoredValue property wrapper persists a single Item in the system Keychain
func set(_ value: Item?) throws — writes the value to the Keychain
func remove() throws — removes the value from the Keychain
typed wrapper for the keychain Service; KeychainGroup and KeychainError also public
Package.swift declares .iOS(.v17) platform floor

notes: Store/StoredValue/SecurelyStoredValue are @Observable; change tracking via AsyncStream events plus Observation framework integration.

evidence.note: var events: AsyncStream<StoreEvent<Item>> — granular events tracking API for all Store changes
event type carrying operation (.initialized/.loaded/.insert/.remove) and affected items
AsyncStream that emits all value changes of a @StoredValue
AsyncStream that emits all value changes of a @SecurelyStoredValue
SwiftUI View hook that fires when a Store finishes loading
